application.title: Application
application.description: Application settings. Change in config.toml and restart to take effect.
application.themeDescription: Choose between light, dark, or follow system theme.
application.languageDescription: Choose the language used in the WebUI.
application.updateEnabled: You will now be notified of new updates.
application.updateDisabled: You will no longer be notified of new updates.

account.title: Account
account.updateSuccess: User updated successfully. Please sign in again!
account.updateError: Error updating credentials. Did you provide the correct current password?
account.changeCredentialsDescription: The username and password can be changed either separately or simultaneously. Note that you will be logged out after changing credentials.
account.oidcDescription: Your account credentials are managed by your OpenID Connect provider. To change your username, please visit your provider's settings page and log in again.

logsPage.title: Logs
logsPage.description: Configure log level, log size rotation, etc. You can download your old log files below.

irc.addFailed: IRC Network could not be added

irc.channelsUpdateDesc: Channels are added when you setup IRC indexers. Do not edit unless you know what you are doing.

releases.warningBody: This section manages release history deletion. Automated cleanup jobs run on schedules, while manual deletion allows immediate one-time cleanup. Both operations permanently delete data and cannot be undone.
releases.olderThanDesc: How old releases must be before deletion (Required for both automated and manual deletion)
releases.indexersDesc: Optional filter (if none selected, applies to all indexers)
releases.releaseStatusesDesc: Optional filter (if none selected, applies to all release statuses)
releases.cleanupJobsDesc: Schedule automatic cleanup of old releases with custom filters.
releases.name: Name
releases.deleteReleaseHistoryDesc: Select the criteria below to permanently delete release history records that are older than the chosen age and optionally match the selected indexers and release statuses:
releases.invalidAgeExample: Please enter a valid age. For example, 6 months or 1 year.
releases.allDeleted: All releases based on criteria were deleted.
releases.removeCleanupJobText: Are you sure you want to remove this cleanup job? This action cannot be undone.

duplicateFieldTitles.hash: Normalized hash of the release name. Use with Release name for exact match
duplicateFieldTitles.title: Parsed title
duplicateFieldTitles.subTitle: Parsed sub title like Episode name
duplicateFieldTitles.edition: Edition (eg. Collectors Edition) and Cut (eg. Directors Cut)

releases.removeReleasesScopeAll: delete all release history records for {{indexers}}{{statuses}}
releases.removeReleasesScopeOlder: permanently delete all release history records older than {{duration}} for {{indexers}}{{statuses}}
releases.removeReleasesStatuses: and with the following release statuses: {{statuses}}

downloadClient.removeText: Are you sure you want to remove this download client? This action cannot be undone.
downloadClient.name: Name
downloadClient.authTypeTooltip: This should in most cases be Basic Auth, but some providers use Digest Auth.
downloadClient.guidesDeluge: See guides for how to connect to Deluge for various server types in our docs.
downloadClient.guidesArr: See guides for how to connect to the *arr suite for various server types in our docs.
downloadClient.guidesQbit: See guides for how to connect to qBittorrent for various server types in our docs.
downloadClient.guidesRTorrent: See guides for how to connect to rTorrent for various server types in our docs.
downloadClient.guidesTransmission: See guides for how to connect to Transmission for various server types in our docs.
downloadClient.guidesSabnzbd: See our guides on how to connect to SABnzbd for various server types in our docs.
downloadClient.maxActiveDownloadsTooltip: Limit the amount of active downloads (0 is unlimited), to give the maximum amount of bandwidth and disk for the downloads.
downloadClient.seeRecommendations: See recommendations for various server types here:
downloadClient.downloadClientDescription: Override download client to use. Can also be overridden per Filter Action.
downloadClient.clientNameTooltip: Specify what client the arr should use by default. Can be overridden per filter action.
downloadClient.clientIdDeprecatedTooltip: DEPRECATED: Use Client name field instead.
downloadClient.ignoreConditionTooltip: Choose whether to respect or ignore the Max active downloads setting before checking speed thresholds.
downloadClient.downloadSpeedThresholdHelp: If download speed is below this when max active downloads is hit, download anyways. KB/s
downloadClient.uploadSpeedThresholdHelp: If upload speed is below this when max active downloads is hit, download anyways. KB/s

feed.title: Feed
feed.name: Name
feed.warningTitle: Warning: Indexers might ban you for too low interval!
feed.torznabUrlHelp: Torznab URL. Use only the URL without extra params.
feed.newznabUrlHelp: Newznab URL. Use only the URL without extra params.
feed.urlFormatTitle: Prowlarr and Jackett have different formats:
feed.refreshIntervalHelp: Minutes. Recommended 15-30. Too low may risk a ban.
feed.refreshTimeoutHelp: Seconds to wait before cancelling refresh.
feed.maxAgeHelp: Maximum age of feed content in seconds. Use 0 to disable the age filter and process all items.
feed.categoriesDescription: Fetch available categories and select what to include.

list.name: Name
list.description: Auto update filters from lists and arrs.
list.filtersDescription: Select filters to update for this list.
list.removeText: Are you sure you want to remove {{name}}? This action cannot be undone.
list.arrSourceDescription: Update filters from titles in Radarr, Sonarr, Lidarr, Readarr, or Whisparr.
list.traktSourceDescription: Use a Trakt list or one of the default autobrr hosted lists.
list.anilistSourceDescription: Use an AniList list from one of the default autobrr hosted lists.
list.plaintextSourceDescription: Use a plain text list with one item per line.
list.metacriticSourceDescription: Use a Metacritic list or one of the default autobrr hosted lists.
list.mdblistSourceDescription: Use a MDBList list or one of the default autobrr hosted lists.
list.traktHelp: Default Trakt lists. Override with your own.
list.traktApiKeyHelp: Trakt API Key. Required for private lists.
list.matchReleaseDesc: Use Match Releases field. Uses Movies/Shows field by default.
list.includeUnmonitoredDesc: By default only monitored titles are filtered.
list.includeAlternateTitlesDesc: Include alternate titles in the filter.
list.skipCleanSanitize: Bypass cleanup and sanitization and use the list as-is
list.skipCleanSanitizeDesc: By default, titles are automatically sanitized and checked for unusual characters.
list.metacriticHelp: Metacritic lists. Override with your own.
list.mdblistHelp: MDBLists.com lists. Override with your own.
list.includeYearDesc: Include the release year in the filter for movies. Requires Match Releases. Example: Movie?Title?2024*
list.plaintextUrlHelp: URL to a plain text file with one item per line
list.plaintextTooltip1: Plaintext lists can read from both HTTP URLs and local files on disk.

notification.settingsDescNotifiarr: Enable the autobrr integration and optionally create a new API Key.
notification.settingsDescLunasea1: LunaSea offers notifications across all devices linked to your account (User-Based) or to a single device without an account, using a unique webhook per device (Device-Based).
notification.settingsDescPushoverSuffix: and add its API Token here.
notification.settingsDescWebhook: Send a generic autobrr JSON payload to a user-defined webhook URL.
notification.title: Notification
notification.name: Name
notification.globalEventsDesc: Select default events that trigger globally. These can be overridden on a per-filter basis. Leave all unchecked to use this service only for filter-specific notifications.
notification.perFilterEventsDesc: This notification is used in these filters.
notification.telegramProxyHelp: Reverse proxy domain for api.telegram.org. Only needed if your network blocks the Telegram API.
notification.senderHelp: Custom sender name shown at the top of a notification
notification.accessTokenHelp: Access token. Use this or Username + password
notification.httpMethodTooltip: HTTP method for the webhook request. Defaults to POST.

indexer.title: Indexer
indexer.baseUrlHelp: Override base URL if it's blocked by your ISP.
indexer.ircDesc: Networks and channels are configured automatically in the background.
indexer.settingsIrcDesc: Networks and channels are configured automatically in the background.
indexer.ircGuideTooltip: Please read our IRC guide if you are unfamiliar with IRC.

indexer.newznabUrlHelp: Newznab URL. Use only the URL without extra params.
indexer.urlFormatTitle: Prowlarr and Jackett have different formats:

indexer.fetchNeedsUrl: Enter a URL to fetch categories
indexer.externalIdentifierHelp: External Identifier for ARRs. If using Prowlarr set like: {{name}} (Prowlarr)
indexer.externalIdentifierTooltip1: External Identifier for use with ARRs to get features like seed limits working.
indexer.externalIdentifierTooltip2: This needs to match the indexer name in your ARR. If using Prowlarr it will likely be "{{name}} (Prowlarr)"
indexer.secretFieldTooltip: This field does not take a full URL. Only use alphanumeric strings like uqcdi67cibkx3an8cmdm.
indexer.proxyDesc: Set a proxy to be used for downloads of .torrent files and feeds.

cleanupJob.title: Cleanup Job
cleanupJob.scheduleHelp: Cron expression. Examples: '0 3 * * *' = daily 3 AM, '0 */6 * * *' = every 6 hours
cleanupJob.olderThanHelp: Delete releases older than this duration
cleanupJob.leaveEmptyIndexers: Leave empty to apply to all indexers
cleanupJob.leaveEmptyStatuses: Leave empty to apply to all statuses

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: Configure notification services and their global event triggers.
notifications.infoBody: You can override these global settings for specific filters in each filter's Notifications tab. Global events are optional - you can create notification services without any events and configure them only at the filter level. System events (IRC disconnected/reconnected, app updates) always use global settings.

indexers.title: Indexers
indexers.description: Indexer settings for IRC, RSS, Newznab, and Torznab based indexers.
indexers.descriptionGeneric: Generic RSS/Newznab/Torznab feeds can be added here by selecting one of the Generic indexers.

proxies.description: Proxies that can be used with Indexers, feeds and IRC.

feeds.forceRunText: Are you sure you want to force run the {{name}} feed? Respecting RSS interval rules is crucial to avoid potential IP bans.
